mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains at or near zero while CPI inflation accelerates above 7% year-over-year, signaling real rate compression that erodes bond yields and redirects capital into equities seeking real returns. This mechanism operates continuously in low-rate, high-inflation regimes and predicts positive SPX returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 287.708 (8.5% YoY) as of March 2022
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.5%, t-test p > 0.10) when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% and CPIAUCSL YoY growth > 6%, measured over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate falls below 4% while payroll employment growth remains positive, indicating a tight labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings. This always-on signal predicts positive SPX returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 150,925 thousand as of March 2022
failure_condition: Rank IC between (UNRATE, PAYEMS) and 5-day SPX returns is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 5-day returns in tight labor market periods (UNRATE < 4%) do not exceed baseline by at least 0.3% (t-test p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month by more than 1% while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1.5 million annualized units, triggering a demand shock signal that predicts negative SPX returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM decline > 1% AND HOUST < 1500 thousand units
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 665,726 million as of February 2022
fact:HOUST at 1,769 thousand as of February 2022
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive (mean > 0.2%) in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window when both triggers are active.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) decelerates to growth rates below 0.5% month-over-month for two consecutive months, signaling supply-side weakness that compresses profit margins and predicts negative SPX returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices cyclical earnings downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM growth < 0.5% for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following supply deceleration trigger are positive (mean > 1%) in more than 60% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate rises above 1.5% in a single month (signaling accelerated tightening), triggering a regime shift from accommodation to restriction. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative SPX returns over 20 trading days as equity risk premiums expand and growth expectations compress.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive (mean > 0.5%) in more than 65% of instances when FEDFUNDS jumps > 1.5% in a month, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) is positive and accelerating quarter-over-quarter while unemployment rate falls, indicating robust demand expansion. This always-on signal in growth regimes predicts positive SPX returns over 60 trading days as earnings revisions flow upward and equity valuations expand.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19,806.29 billion (2017 dollars) as of Q4 2021
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are negative (mean < -0.5%) in more than 50% of periods where GDPC1 growth is positive and UNRATE < 4%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points in a single month while payroll employment declines, signaling an abrupt labor market deterioration that triggers recession fears. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative SPX returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices tail risk upward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in single month AND PAYEMS declines MoM
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 150,925 thousand as of March 2022
regime_hint: recession onset or labor market shock
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive (mean > 0.3%) in more than 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) accelerates to growth rates above 1.5% month-over-month while payroll employment grows above 200 thousand per month, indicating synchronized supply and labor expansion that supports margin expansion and predicts positive SPX returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 150,925 thousand as of March 2022
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns are negative (mean < -0.3%) in more than 55% of periods where INDPRO MoM growth > 1.5% and PAYEMS growth > 200 thousand, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
